value: Hacktivism / volunteer-botnet case (NoName057(16) DDoSia). This is a crowdsourced DDoS toolkit, not a stealth intrusion, and its network indicators DECAY fast: Recorded Future assessed Tier-1 C2 servers rotate with an average lifespan of ~9 days, and client build hashes change per release. The hard hashes and the C2 IP below are the PUBLISHED, HISTORICALLY-ANALYZED 2023 build (Sekoia TDR) kept as reference/genealogy anchors - do NOT treat them as currently-live. The DURABLE detection is behavioral: the /client/login and /client/get_targets C2 handshake, the Go-http-client User-Agent, and the Client-Hash/User-Hash request headers. Pull live C2 fresh from ThreatFox / SEKOIA Community before blocking. No CVE is associated with this case.

DDoSia Tier-1 C2 (port 80) - HISTORICAL 2023 build; DECAYED, ~9-day server lifespan, do NOT block as live. Pattern anchor only.
DDoSia C2 authentication endpoint (POST). Durable behavioral anchor - client posts User-Hash + Client-Hash to authenticate and receive a token.
DDoSia C2 target-list endpoint (GET). Durable behavioral anchor - client pulls the AES-GCM-encrypted target list keyed off the returned token.
Default User-Agent of the DDoSia Go client on the C2 handshake (not the flood traffic, which uses configured/randomized headers). Combine with the /client/* URIs to reduce FP.
Custom HTTP request header sent by the DDoSia client - SHA256 of the machine UID concatenated with the client PID. Durable protocol tell.
Custom HTTP request header carrying the bcrypt-form volunteer id (starts $2a$16$) issued by @DDosiabot in client_id.txt.
Bcrypt hash prefix that opens the volunteer client_id.txt / User-Hash header (work factor 16).
Volunteer identity file issued by @DDosiabot; must sit in the same folder as the client binary. Strong host artifact.
Archive distributed to volunteers containing the six per-OS client binaries and help.txt.
Console banner string printed by the DDoSia client (Go-Stresser <ver> | PID <n> (c) NoName057(16)).
Group self-branding embedded in the client banner and Telegram channels.
Canonical DDoSia client filename (per-OS set: d_linux_amd64, d_linux_arm, d_mac_amd64, d_mac_arm64, d_windows_amd64.exe, d_windows_arm64.exe).
Telegram registration bot that issues client_id.txt and TON-wallet crypto rewards to volunteers.
Field in the decrypted DDoSia target JSON (alongside target_id, request_id, host, ip, type, method, port, path, body). Appears in memory / decrypted config, not the on-disk binary.

value: C2 handshake shape: POST /client/login (headers User-Hash $2a$16$..., Client-Hash <sha256>:<pid>, User-Agent Go-http-client/1.1, JSON body {location:...}) -> 200 with a numeric token; then GET /client/get_targets (header Time=<token>) -> JSON {token, data} where data is base64 AES-GCM. Key = (token/5)+User-Hash last-32-hex; IV = first 12 bytes of ciphertext; TAG = last 16 bytes.
context: Protocol

value: Victimology (sustained 2026 campaign): government/public sector is the top target (~41% of observed attacks), then transport/logistics (~12%) and tech/media/comms (~10%). 2026 focus: UK critical infrastructure and local government (NCSC warning Jan-2026); Denmark and Greenland after the 24-Mar-2026 snap election (CSIS); NATO eastern flank and Ukraine remain primary. Targeting is reactive to geopolitics.
